description:
Files: src/fz_ir.rs, src/ir_typer.rs, src/ir_lower.rs

IR change:
  Term::TailCall { callee: FnId, args: Vec<Var>, is_back_edge: bool }

Expose scc_of from typer (currently computed but discarded at ir_typer.rs:607):
  pub struct ModuleTypes { ..., pub scc_of: HashMap<FnId, usize> }
Populate in type_module where 'let _ = scc_of' currently appears.

In ir_lower, thread &ModuleTypes through lower_expr context. At Term::TailCall site:
  is_back_edge = scc_of.get(&callee) == scc_of.get(&ctx.current_fn_id)

Self-recursion is the degenerate SCC-of-one case. Mutual recursion (f→g→f) covered automatically. Non-recursive calls get is_back_edge=false.

If is_back_edge && args.len() > 8: return LowerError::BackEdgeTooManyArgs { span, fn_name, callee_name, arg_count }. This is a programming error at the declaration — emit structured diagnostic, not a runtime assert.

Tests: self-recursive fn → is_back_edge=true. Mutual recursion pair → both cross-calls true. Non-recursive call → false. 9-arg back-edge → LowerError. Existing lowering tests pass with is_back_edge:false in match arms.
title: fz-sched.8: SCC-based is_back_edge on Term::TailCall; >8-arg compile error
